title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure could lead to significant disruptions, including data encryption, service outages, financial losses, and reputational damage. The use of compromised CI/CD pipelines increases the risk of widespread propagation and supply chain attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately audit and secure CI/CD pipelines by rotating credentials, implementing least privilege access, and monitoring for unauthorized changes.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and enable logging to detect and respond to anomalous activity in cloud environments.
Conduct regular backups of critical data and ensure backups are stored offline or in a secure, immutable environment.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A weakness has been identified in CoreWorxLab CAAL up to 1.6.0. The affected element is an...
description: A weakness has been identified in CoreWorxLab CAAL up to 1.6.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file src/caal/webhooks.py of the component test-hass Endpoint. This manipulation causes server-side request forgery. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified vulnerability in CoreWorxLab CAAL up to version 1.6.0 allows for server-side request forgery (SSRF), which could enable attackers to manipulate server requests, access internal systems, or exfiltrate sensitive data. The public availability of the exploit increases the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of CoreWorxLab CAAL if available, or apply mitigations such as disabling the vulnerable endpoint.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization on all endpoints to prevent malicious requests.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity, especially requests targeting the 'test-hass Endpoint', and deploy intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Dataease 2.10.20. Impacted is the function SqlparserUtils...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Dataease 2.10.20. Impacted is the function SqlparserUtils.transFilter of the file SqlparserUtils.java of the component Data Dashboard. The manipulation results in sql injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8724.

title: Das U-Boot before 2026.04 allows FIT (Flat Image Tree) signature verification bypass because...
description: Das U-Boot before 2026.04 allows FIT (Flat Image Tree) signature verification bypass because hashed-nodes is omitted from a hash.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Das U-Boot allows attackers to bypass FIT signature verification, potentially enabling unauthorized code execution or firmware modification. This could compromise the integrity and security of embedded systems, leading to data breaches, device malfunction, or exploitation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Das U-Boot version 2026.04 or later to ensure the vulnerability is patched.
Implement additional integrity checks for firmware and bootloader images to mitigate risks.
Monitor systems for signs of unauthorized firmware modifications and apply security updates promptly.

title: WordPress Plugin Backup and Restore 1.0.3 contains an arbitrary file deletion vulnerability that...
description: WordPress Plugin Backup and Restore 1.0.3 contains an arbitrary file deletion vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to delete files by manipulating parameters in AJAX requests. Attackers can send POST requests to admin-ajax.php with crafted file_name and folder_name parameters to delete arbitrary files from the WordPress installation directory.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers to delete arbitrary files from the WordPress installation directory, potentially leading to website defacement, loss of critical data, or disruption of services.
recommended_actions: Update the WordPress Plugin Backup and Restore to the latest patched version immediately if available.
Restrict access to admin-ajax.php by implementing strong authentication measures and limiting access to trusted users.
Regularly back up website files and databases to ensure recovery in case of file deletion.

title: Quick.CMS 6.7 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability in the sliders form that allows...
description: Quick.CMS 6.7 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability in the sliders form that allows authenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts by submitting XSS payloads through the sDescription parameter. Attackers can craft CSRF forms targeting the admin.php?p=sliders-form endpoint to execute arbitrary JavaScript in victim browsers when the form is submitted.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47981.

title: WP Learn Manager 1.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows...
description: WP Learn Manager 1.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts through the fieldtitle parameter. Attackers can submit POST requests to the jslm_fieldordering page with XSS payloads in the fieldtitle field to execute arbitrary JavaScript when administrators view the field ordering interface.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47975.

Attackers can generate a payload containing 350000 repeated characters and paste it twice into a new note to trigger an application crash.

title: CouchCMS 2.2.1 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers...
description: CouchCMS 2.2.1 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript by uploading malicious SVG files through the file upload functionality. Attackers can upload SVG files containing embedded script tags to the browse.php endpoint, which are then executed in users' browsers when the files are accessed or previewed.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47955.

description: Kite 4.2.0.1 U1 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in the KiteService Windows service that allows local attackers to escalate privileges by exploiting the service binary path. Attackers can place a malicious executable in the Program Files directory to be executed with LocalSystem privileges when the service starts.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2020-37247.

title: python jsonpickle 2.0.0 contains a remote code execution vulnerability that allows attackers to...
description: python jsonpickle 2.0.0 contains a remote code execution vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary Python commands by deserializing malicious JSON payloads containing py/repr objects. Attackers can craft JSON strings with py/repr directives that invoke the eval function during deserialization to execute system commands and arbitrary code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47952.

title: WordPress Plugin Anti-Malware Security and Bruteforce Firewall 4.20.59 contains a directory...
description: WordPress Plugin Anti-Malware Security and Bruteforce Firewall 4.20.59 contains a directory traversal vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to read arbitrary files by manipulating the file parameter. Attackers can send requests to the duplicator_download action via admin-ajax.php with path traversal sequences to access sensitive system files outside the intended directory.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The directory traversal vulnerability in the WordPress Plugin Anti-Malware Security and Bruteforce Firewall 4.20.59 allows unauthenticated attackers to access sensitive files on the server. This could lead to data breaches, exposure of configuration files, credentials, or other sensitive information, and potentially enable further exploitation of the system.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the WordPress plugin to the latest patched version or apply any available security fixes provided by the plugin developer.
Implement web application firewall (WAF) rules to block requests containing suspicious path traversal sequences.
Review server logs for any signs of exploitation attempts and secure sensitive files by restricting access permissions.

title: Sticky Notes Widget 3.0.6 contains a denial of service vulnerability that allows attackers to...
description: Sticky Notes Widget 3.0.6 contains a denial of service vulnerability that allows attackers to crash the application by pasting excessively long character strings into note fields. Attackers can generate a payload containing 350000 repeated characters and paste it twice into a new note to trigger an application crash on iOS devices.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Sticky Notes Widget 3.0.6 allows attackers to crash the application on iOS devices, leading to a denial of service. While the impact is limited to application availability and does not compromise sensitive data, it can disrupt user productivity and potentially affect businesses relying on the app.
recommended_actions: Update Sticky Notes Widget to the latest patched version as soon as a fix is available.
Implement input validation to restrict excessively long character strings in note fields.
Monitor application logs for unusual activity, such as repeated crashes, to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: TextPattern CMS 4.9.0-dev contains a remote code execution vulnerability that allows...
description: TextPattern CMS 4.9.0-dev contains a remote code execution vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to upload arbitrary PHP files by exploiting the plugin upload functionality. Attackers can authenticate, retrieve a CSRF token from the plugin event page, and upload malicious PHP files to the textpattern/tmp/ directory for code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47976.

title: Home Assistant Community Store (HACS) 1.10.0 contains a path traversal vulnerability that allows...
description: Home Assistant Community Store (HACS) 1.10.0 contains a path traversal vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to read sensitive files by traversing directories via the /hacsfiles/ endpoint. Attackers can retrieve the .storage/auth file containing user credentials and refresh tokens, then craft valid JWT tokens to gain administrative access to Home Assistant instances.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to access sensitive files, including user credentials and refresh tokens, enabling them to craft valid JWT tokens and gain administrative access to Home Assistant instances. This could lead to unauthorized control of smart home devices, data breaches, and potential lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Home Assistant Community Store (HACS) to the latest patched version to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the /hacsfiles/ endpoint using firewall rules or web server configurations to prevent unauthorized access.
Rotate all user credentials and refresh tokens to invalidate any potentially compromised tokens and monitor for unauthorized access.

description: Macaron Notes 5.5 contains a denial of service vulnerability that allows attackers to crash the application by creating notes with excessively long character strings. Attackers can generate a payload containing 350000 repeated characters and paste it into a note field to trigger application crash and stop functionality.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47970.

title: Cookie Law Bar 1.2.1 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows...
description: Cookie Law Bar 1.2.1 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts by submitting unsanitized input to the Bar Message field. Attackers can inject script payloads through the plugin settings page that execute in the browsers of all WordPress users viewing the site, enabling cookie theft and sensitive data exfiltration.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into the Cookie Law Bar plugin, potentially leading to cookie theft, session hijacking, and exfiltration of sensitive user data. This could compromise user accounts and damage the reputation of affected websites.
recommended_actions: Update the Cookie Law Bar plugin to the latest patched version or apply any available security patches immediately.
Sanitize and validate all user inputs in the plugin settings to prevent injection of malicious scripts.
Implement a Content Security Policy (CSP) to mitigate the impact of potential XSS attacks.

title: EgavilanMedia PHPCRUD 1.0 contains an SQL injection vulnerability that allows unauthenticated...
description: EgavilanMedia PHPCRUD 1.0 contains an SQL injection vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to manipulate database queries by injecting SQL code through the firstname parameter. Attackers can send POST requests to insert.php with malicious firstname values to extract sensitive database information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47956.

title: Supsystic Pricing Table 1.8.7 contains an SQL injection vulnerability in the 'sidx' GET parameter...
description: Supsystic Pricing Table 1.8.7 contains an SQL injection vulnerability in the 'sidx' GET parameter that allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL queries through the getListForTbl action. The plugin also contains stored cross-site scripting vulnerabilities in the 'Edit name' and 'Edit HTML' fields that execute malicious scripts when viewing pricing tables.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in the 'sidx' GET parameter allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL queries, potentially leading to data breaches, unauthorized access, or database manipulation. The stored cross-site scripting vulnerabilities could enable attackers to inject malicious scripts, leading to phishing attacks, session hijacking, or malware distribution.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Supsystic Pricing Table plugin to the latest patched version, if available, or disable it temporarily.
Implement web application firewall (WAF) rules to detect and block SQL injection and XSS attempts targeting this plugin.
Conduct a comprehensive security audit of your website to identify and mitigate any other vulnerabilities.

title: VX Search 13.5.28 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in both VX Search Server and VX...
description: VX Search 13.5.28 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in both VX Search Server and VX Search Enterprise services that allows local attackers to escalate privileges. Attackers can place malicious executables in unquoted path directories like C:\Program Files\VX Search to execute arbitrary code with LocalSystem privileges when services restart.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47974.

title: CVE-2026-7258 - In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.
description: In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.6, some functions, including urldecode(), pass signed char to ctype functions (like isxdigit()). On the systems with default signed char and optimized table-lookup ctype functions - such as NetBSD - this can lead to accessing array with negative offset, which can trigger a denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in specific PHP versions can lead to a denial of service (DoS) attack by exploiting functions like urldecode() that pass signed char to ctype functions. This could allow attackers to crash applications or services relying on vulnerable PHP versions, potentially disrupting operations and causing downtime.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade PHP to the latest patched versions: 8.2.31, 8.3.31, 8.4.21, or 8.5.6, depending on the version in use.
Conduct a thorough review of applications using the affected PHP versions to identify and mitigate potential exposure to the vulnerability.
Implement web application firewalls (WAF) to monitor and block malicious traffic targeting this vulnerability until patches are applied.

title: CVE-2026-6735 - In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, 8.5.* before 8.5.6, d
description: In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, 8.5.* before 8.5.6, due to improper sanitation of user data, it allows an attacker to compose an URL, which will cause the target to execute arbitrary JavaScript code (XSS) on the target's machine when the target is viewing the PHP-FPM status page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6735.

title: CVE-2026-6722 - In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.
description: In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.6, the SOAP extension's object deduplication mechanism stores pointers to PHP objects in a global map without incrementing their reference counts. When an apache:Map node contains duplicate keys, processing the second entry overwrites the first in the temporary result map, freeing the original PHP object while its stale pointer remains in the map. A subsequent href reference to the freed node can copy the dangling pointer into the result. As PHP string allocations can reclaim the freed memory region, an attacker with control over the SOAP request body can exploit this use-after-free to achieve remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6722.

title: CVE-2025-14179 - In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.
description: In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.6, the PDO Firebird driver improperly handles NUL bytes when preparing SQL queries. During token-by-token query construction, a string token containing a NUL byte is copied via strncat(), which stops at the NUL byte, dropping the closing quote and causing subsequent SQL tokens to be interpreted as part of the string. This allows SQL injection when attacker-controlled values are quoted via PDO::quote() and embedded in SQL statements.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2025-14179.

title: CVE-2026-8224 - A vulnerability was determined in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected by this issue is the function pcf_se
description: A vulnerability was determined in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected by this issue is the function pcf_sess_set_ipv6prefix of the file /src/pcf/context.c of the component PCF. Executing a manipulation of the argument SmPolicyContextData.ipv6AddressPrefix can lead to denial of service. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8224.
